mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 8.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit margins, directly causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or the signal's out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean 20-day return when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production signals supply-side normalization and capacity utilization recovery, triggering short-term equity rallies as investors price in improved operational efficiency.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in 70% of months following an INDPRO increase, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter expansion in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher aggregate corporate earnings, establishing a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth rates (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year inflation rate crossing above 3.0 percent forces central bank tightening expectations, which compresses equity valuation multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0 threshold, or the signal's Sharpe ratio turns negative out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls and increases in the unemployment rate confirm structural labor market deterioration, which reduces aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a positive mean 20-day return when both labor indicators move adversely, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Peak and reversal in housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signals demand exhaustion and reduced credit availability, which precedes negative short-term S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 50% of observations following a housing starts peak, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in backtesting.
